supply_chain.dependencies: Consolidation of qualified shipyard facilities could result in higher prices for construction of new ships and limit availability. Limited capacity and availability of shipyards and related subcontractors, including lack of viable dry-dock facilities in Western Hemisphere. Single vendor provides hotel and restaurant services including food and labor costs.
supply_chain.risks: Consolidation of control of certain European cruise shipyards could result in higher prices and limited availability. Lack of qualified shipyard repair facilities could result in inability to repair and maintain ships on a timely basis. Delays in shipbuilding program due to work stoppages, insolvency, force majeure events, regulatory changes, sustainability initiatives. Supply chain disruptions from global events including armed conflicts and pandemics.

regulatory_risks: International Maritime Organization (IMO) environmental regulations including MARPOL
EU Emissions Trading System (ETS) - maritime sector inclusion effective January 2024
FuelEU Maritime Initiative - GHG intensity limits starting 2025
Carbon Intensity Indicator (CII) requirements - annual ratings from A to E
Energy Efficiency Ship Index (EEXI) requirements
EPA Vessel General Permit (VGP) requirements
Vessel Incidental Discharge Act (VIDA) implementation
Oil Pollution Act of 1990 (OPA 90) compliance
State of Alaska environmental legislation and discharge restrictions
EU regulations on sulfur emissions and fuel requirements
Shore power connection requirements in EU ports by 2030
SOLAS safety and security requirements
International Port and Ship Facility Code (ISPS Code)
Maritime Transportation Security Act of 2002 (MTSA)
Maritime Labor Convention (MLC 2006) requirements
Federal Maritime Commission (FMC) financial responsibility requirements
UK consumer protection and financial security requirements
CDC health and safety requirements
FDA food and water safety regulations
Data privacy and protection laws in multiple jurisdictions
Payment Card Industry security requirements
OECD BEPS 2.0 Pillar 2 global tax reform - 15% minimum tax
Bermuda Corporate Income Tax Act 2023 - 15% tax effective January 2025
Economic substance requirements in Bermuda, Guernsey, Isle of Man, British Virgin Islands, Bahamas, Saint Lucia
Port capacity constraints and local government restrictions on cruise operations
Environmental regulations aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions
Increased government taxes, fees and port expenses
Potential new taxes or fees specifically applicable to cruise industry

sanction_risks: Armed conflicts and geopolitical tensions impacting operations and itineraries
Government sanctions limiting destinations and operations
Trade disputes and increased tariffs affecting costs
Political instability in various countries affecting port availability
Regulatory changes due to geopolitical developments
Difficulty sourcing crew members, guests and vendors from affected regions
Supply chain disruptions from armed conflicts
Volatility in global financial markets from geopolitical tensions
Increased fuel and commodity pricing from conflicts
Helms-Burton Act litigation related to Cuban operations
